title: #eng-releases: 1740401120
preview:
Connor O'Brien: Drafting canary plan for runtime 1.19 TP stability work. Want to lock: (1) which clusters/accounts, (2) gating checks, (3) rollback triggers, (4) who’s on point.

Connor O'Brien: Proposed build: `runtime-1.19.0-rc3` (includes PRs 21841/21853/21860/21866/21875/21912/21921 + dashboard PR 412). Dedicated-first per ENG-4829.

Connor O'Brien: Canary scope proposal:
1) Dedicated (TP-heavy):
- `dedicated-us-east-1/acme-ai-prod` (A100 80GB, TP=4 replicas) — 10% of replicas first (pin subset), then 50%, then 100%
- `dedicated-us-west-2/helios-search-stage` (H100, mixed load) — 25% replicas (stage only)
2) Hosted:
- `hosted-us-west-2/canary-pool-a` — 1% traffic for TP routes only (router rule)
- `hosted-eu-central-1/canary-pool-b` — 0.5% traffic, TP only
3) Internal perf-canary:
- benchmark-lab TP stress scenarios (long context + bursty concurrency) must pass before step 2 (Hosted)

Connor O'Brien: Feature flags (start conservative):
- `tp.scheduler_guardrail` = ON
- `tp.collective_stream_sync` = ON (A100/H100 only)
- `tp.watchdog` = ON but high thresholds (per model preset)
- `tp.load_shed_on_stall` = ON for canary pools
- `tp.concurrency_caps_defaults` = ON for canary only

Connor O'Brien: Gating checks (first 2h + 24h soak):
- No increase in request timeouts (p95/p99) > 10% vs baseline for same account/cluster
- TP heartbeat gaps: p99 gap < 2s; no sustained gaps > 10s on any rank
- Stall reason counters: no sustained growth in `stall_reason=collective_wait` or `scheduler_starvation`
- Watchdog abort rate < 0.02% of TP requests (and must drain cleanly; no wedge)
- NCCL errors: zero (or explainable transient on drain)
- Replica drain events: not > 2x baseline (avoid thrash)

Connor O'Brien: Rollback triggers (immediate):
- Any confirmed GPU wedge / stuck collectives (SRE declares) OR watchdog triggers but replica doesn’t drain within 90s
- `tp_watchdog_abort` > 0.1% over 15m on a canary pool
- p99 latency regression > 20% for 30m on Acme OR error budget burn > 2%/hr (SLO dashboard)
- Spike in `nccl_timeout` / `collective_mismatch` surfaced to API

Connor O'Brien: Rollback action ladder:
1) Flip off `tp.collective_stream_sync` + `tp.watchdog` (keep scheduler guardrail if safe)
2) Drain canary replicas + revert router rules back to baseline pools
3) If still unstable, rollback runtime to 1.18 on affected cluster

Connor O'Brien: Who’s on point:
- Release DRI: me
- Runtime tech: @Noah Patel
- SRE DRI for canary window: @Rafael Mendes (backup @Sean Gallagher)
- Dedicated comms: @Lauren Bishop / @Paula Mendes
- Support escalation: @Owen Phillips

Connor O'Brien: Please poke holes / confirm cluster list + thresholds. Goal: start Dedicated canary tomorrow 10:00–13:00 PT.

Rafael Mendes: Looks mostly good. Two comments:
1) I want the “sustained heartbeat gap >10s” to page immediately during the window, not just a gate. It’s the earliest signal we’ve had.
2) For Acme, 10% replicas is fine but *pin to a specific AZ* if we can. We don’t want cross-AZ variance muddying.

Aisha Bello: +1. We can page on `tp_rank_heartbeat_gap_seconds{p99} > 5` for 5m to start (warning) and `>10` for 2m (page) during canary only. After rollout we can tune.

Noah Patel: Cluster list ok. One nuance: for `tp.watchdog` thresholds, we should start with model presets (70B > 13B). The false positive risk is real on long context + heavy prefill.

Noah Patel: Also for rollback ladder, I’d rather *disable watchdog last*, not first. If we think a deadlock is in play, watchdog is the mechanism that gets us out without manual intervention. Disabling stream sync first makes sense.

Sofia Ivanova: Re scheduler guardrail: should be ON in all canary steps. If we turn it off as a rollback step, we may reintroduce starvation and end up in the same hang signature.

Hiro Tanaka: For stream/event sync hardening: we should keep it ON for A100/H100 canary, but if we see perf regression, we can switch to “conditional sync only around collectives” (flag variant). Do we have that knob exposed?

Connor O'Brien: We have `tp.collective_stream_sync.mode = {always, collectives_only}` in rc3. I’ll add it to the plan and default to `collectives_only` for Hosted canary to reduce perf hit.

Sergio Costa: Dashboard is ready in the canary folder. Panels include: heartbeat gaps by rank, stall_reason breakdown, request timeouts by pool, drain events, and NCCL errors. Link: confluence “TP stall + timeout dashboard” (same as PR-412 notes).

Sean Gallagher: +1 to keeping watchdog ON. Our failure mode is “wedged replica blocks queue -> pileup -> autoscaler thrash”. Watchdog + load-shed is how we contain blast radius.

Sean Gallagher: For rollback triggers, add “autoscaler thrash / replica churn > X” as an explicit trigger. That’s what customer feels (timeouts). Even if p99 doesn’t move immediately, churn is bad.

Rafael Mendes: Agree. We can use `replica_restarts_per_hour` + `drain_events` as proxy. If either > 3x baseline for 30m, we pause rollout.

Marissa Cole: For Hosted: can we start at 0.5% in one region only for the first 6h, then add the second region? I’m trying to keep exposure minimal until we see stable.

Connor O'Brien: Yep. Updated Hosted steps:
- Step H1: us-west-2 only @0.5% TP routes, 6h
- Step H2: add eu-central-1 @0.5%
- Step H3: us-west-2 to 1%

Lauren Bishop: Dedicated comms: Acme expects an update in the morning. If we’re starting 10am PT, I’ll send heads-up that we’ll canary a subset of replicas and expect reduced timeout rates; ask them to keep their current concurrency cap until we say otherwise.

Paula Mendes: +1. Please make sure we don’t promise “fixed” until 24h soak. Phrase it as “stability improvements + active monitoring, rollback ready”.

Owen Phillips: Support side: during canary, if Acme pings with timeouts, what do we want frontline to do? Keep current workaround (concurrency cap) + ask for request IDs + confirm if they hit canary pool.

Noah Patel: Yes. Also ask for whether requests were streaming + any client disconnects (we fixed cancel path in 21890 but still want signal). If they can, capture 1–2 sample prompts lengths + max_tokens.

Connor O'Brien: Noted. I’ll drop a short “support checklist” into the release notes and link the Support Confluence workaround page.

Rafael Mendes: Are we enabling extra NCCL debug logging? If yes, keep it time-bounded. It can be noisy and perf-impacting.

Connor O'Brien: Plan is to enable via INT-2219 for the first 60–90 min of Dedicated step D1 only, then turn off. Only on canary replicas.

DeployBot: Build `runtime-1.19.0-rc3` promoted to canary artifact registry by @Connor O'Brien. (commit: 9f3a2c1)

Hiro Tanaka: One more gate: on the canary replicas, monitor GPU util + SM occupancy; we saw a weird drop when stream sync was misconfigured (ended up serializing). If util drops > 15% without throughput improvement, we should reconsider mode.

Aisha Bello: I can add a temporary panel overlay: throughput tokens/s vs GPU util for canary vs baseline pools.

Connor O'Brien: Updating the final canary doc with these edits:
- Keep watchdog ON; rollback stream sync first
- Add autoscaler thrash/churn trigger
- Hosted staged as Marissa requested
- Add GPU util/throughput gate
- Heartbeat gap paging during window

Connor O'Brien: Final ask: approvals to proceed tomorrow 10am PT? Reply with 👍 / concerns.

Rafael Mendes: 👍 SRE ok. I’ll be on 9:45am PT for preflight.

Noah Patel: 👍

Sofia Ivanova: 👍

Hiro Tanaka: 👍 (please default to `collectives_only` for Hosted)

Marissa Cole: 👍

Lauren Bishop: 👍 I’ll handle Acme comms + keep Support looped.

Sean Gallagher: 👍 (ping me if we see churn / wedge indicators)

Owen Phillips: 👍 I’ll brief frontline + set a watch in the support channel during the window.

Connor O'Brien: Locked. I’ll post preflight checklist + dashboard links in the canary channel in the morning.

title: #eng-releases: 1740086015
preview:
Connor O'Brien: seeing a gap in deploy metadata export -> incident-bot. a bunch of deploy events don’t map to a canonical `service` so the “recent deploys” card is empty for those incidents. anyone else hitting this?

Miles Thornton: yep. in the thread yesterday (#incidents) we had deploy events but bot couldn’t attach them to hosted-api because the event only had image SHA + cluster. no service id.

Sahana Iyer: I pulled a few examples from the export stream. serviceName is inconsistent.

Sahana Iyer: sample payloads (sanitized):
```json
{
  "deploy_id": "dpl_9a1c...",
  "repo": "redwood",
  "commit": "3f2a9b1",
  "image": "registry/redwood/hosted-api:3f2a9b1",
  "cluster": "prod-us-east-1",
  "namespace": "prod",
  "release": "hosted-api",
  "service": null
}
```
vs
```json
{
  "deploy_id": "dpl_0c4e...",
  "repo": "redwood",
  "service": "router",
  "cluster": "prod-us-east-1",
  "namespace": "routing",
  "release": "router"
}
```

Ethan Park: the second one likely came from the control plane pipeline (it knows service). first one is from the old helm deploy step that doesn’t attach service metadata.

Jacob Stein: incident-bot needs a stable key. we can’t keep doing string heuristics on `release` names, esp with dedicated pools where release != service.

Chloe Martin: +1. Console deep links also expect `service_slug` from service registry, otherwise links land on “unknown service” page.

Connor O'Brien: question: what’s the canonical source of truth we should join against? is it service registry or something from the monorepo?

Ethan Park: canonical is service registry. internal name is `svc-registry` (backed by the platform catalog). has mapping from:
- `service_slug` (what Console uses)
- `k8s_namespace` + `k8s_app_label`
- `helm_release`
- `owner_team`
- `repo_path`

Sahana Iyer: do we have an API we can call from deploy exporter to resolve? or do we want to embed a mapping file?

Ethan Park: there’s an API: `GET /v1/resolve` that takes multiple hints and returns canonical service. recommended usage is:
1) try `service_id` if present
2) else `k8s_labels.app` + namespace
3) else `helm_release`
4) else `image_repo` prefix

Miles Thornton: for incident-bot, we only need to display “recent deploys” and link to rollout timeline. if we can resolve to service_slug 95% of time we’re good. degraded mode can show “unresolved deploy” with link to deploy details.

Devon Price: for the bot card, unresolved deploys are currently filtered out (to avoid noise). i can change that to show 1-2 unresolved entries with a warning.

Rafael Mendes: prefer we fix at source so we don’t add bot complexity. but +1 on a safe fallback for first week.

Connor O'Brien: agree. I’d rather the exporter sets `service_slug` on the event. joining later (in bot) means extra runtime dependency during incidents.

Sahana Iyer: concern: service registry call during deploy could block deploy if registry is down.

Ethan Park: resolve endpoint is cached heavily + has an offline fallback (it can return “best guess” from last good snapshot). but we should still make it non-blocking for deploy.

Connor O'Brien: ok so: exporter should call svc-registry, but failure should not fail deploy. it should emit event w/ unresolved fields + `resolve_error`.

Chloe Martin: can we make the join deterministic? if multiple services match same release name that’s dangerous.

Ethan Park: svc-registry’s resolve requires at least 2 signals to disambiguate when collisions exist. e.g. `namespace + release`. if only release is provided and it collides, it returns `ambiguous=true`.

Jacob Stein: we should pass namespace. the exporter already has it.

Sahana Iyer: confirm: for old pipeline events we have `namespace=prod` which is too generic though.

Connor O'Brien: right, `prod` is generic. can we get the actual k8s ns? i think those older helm deploys are in `hosted` namespace not `prod`.

DeployBot: (system) latest deploy step output: `helm --namespace hosted upgrade hosted-api ...`

Connor O'Brien: DeployBot confirms it. so exporter currently writing `namespace=prod` is wrong (or coming from cluster env var). i’ll fix that.

Miles Thornton: that would solve like half our missing mappings.

Sahana Iyer: proposed patch for exporter fields:
- `k8s_namespace`: actual helm namespace
- `helm_release`: helm release
- `k8s_cluster`: existing
- `service_slug`: resolved (optional)

Sahana Iyer: and keep `service` deprecated.

Connor O'Brien: +1. also add `repo` + `commit` (already there). and a stable deploy id.

Chloe Martin: please add `environment` too (prod/stage). Console deep links need env + region.

Ethan Park: svc-registry expects `env` as one of the hints too (some services only exist in stage).

Jacob Stein: for incident-bot, “recent deploys” card should join by service_slug + region. so exporter needs region or cluster->region mapping.

Connor O'Brien: we have cluster name like `prod-us-east-1`. can parse region.

Sahana Iyer: edge case: dedicated deployments where cluster name doesn’t follow that convention.

Ethan Park: dedicated should include explicit `region` tag already (it’s part of cluster metadata). if missing, svc-registry can’t help. but at least we won’t incorrectly map.

Rafael Mendes: what’s the decision here? need something we can write down for the design/ADR.

Connor O'Brien: decision: canonical mapping is svc-registry lookup (service_slug). exporter does lookup best-effort; doesn’t block deploy; emits unresolved+error when cannot resolve. join precedence is service_id > k8s app label+namespace > helm_release+namespace > image repo prefix.

Miles Thornton: and for incident-bot: show unresolved deploys w/ warning + link to deploy details; don’t hide entirely.

Devon Price: ack. i’ll do bot-side as a separate change behind v2 feature flag.

Sahana Iyer: action items:
- Connor: fix exporter to send real helm namespace + call svc-registry resolve; include env/region; log resolve errors
- Ethan: confirm svc-registry resolve contract + ambiguity behavior; share endpoint docs
- Devon: adjust bot card rendering for unresolved deploys (max 2) + add “couldn’t resolve service” hint

Connor O'Brien: I’ll start a PR today. aiming to have this in before the v2 beta cohort expands.

Ethan Park: I’ll drop the docs link here: https://internal.redwood/plat/svc-registry/resolve (requires VPN)

Rafael Mendes: please also add metrics for resolve success rate. we’ll want it in the “context completeness” score.

Connor O'Brien: good call. i’ll add `deploy_export.resolve.success` + `deploy_export.resolve.ambiguous` counters, labeled by env.

Miles Thornton: :thumbsup: once that’s in, we should rerun the Feb incident replay and see if deploy card populates.

Chloe Martin: ping me when you have the final `service_slug` field name. I’ll update Console deep link generator to prefer it.

Connor O'Brien: field name will be `service_slug` (string). consistent with Console.

Sahana Iyer: :+1: done. will update the join plan doc once PR is up.

Sahana Iyer: and keep `service` deprecated.

Connor O'Brien: +1. also add `repo` + `commit` (already there). and a stable deploy id.

Chloe Martin: please add `environment` too (prod/stage). Console deep links need env + region.

Ethan Park: svc-registry expects `env` as one of the hints too (some services only exist in stage).

Jacob Stein: for incident-bot, “recent deploys” card should join by service_slug + region. so exporter needs region or cluster->region mapping.

Connor O'Brien: we have cluster name like `prod-us-east-1`. can parse region.

Sahana Iyer: edge case: dedicated deployments where cluster name doesn’t follow that convention.

Ethan Park: dedicated should include explicit `region` tag already (it’s part of cluster metadata). if missing, svc-registry can’t help. but at least we won’t incorrectly map.

Rafael Mendes: what’s the decision here? need something we can write down for the design/ADR.

Connor O'Brien: decision: canonical mapping is svc-registry lookup (service_slug). exporter does lookup best-effort; doesn’t block deploy; emits unresolved+error when cannot resolve. join precedence is service_id > k8s app label+namespace > helm_release+namespace > image repo prefix.

Miles Thornton: and for incident-bot: show unresolved deploys w/ warning + link to deploy details; don’t hide entirely.

Devon Price: ack. i’ll do bot-side as a separate change behind v2 feature flag.

Sahana Iyer: action items:
- Connor: fix exporter to send real helm namespace + call svc-registry resolve; include env/region; log resolve errors
- Ethan: confirm svc-registry resolve contract + ambiguity behavior; share endpoint docs
- Devon: adjust bot card rendering for unresolved deploys (max 2) + add “couldn’t resolve service” hint

Connor O'Brien: I’ll start a PR today. aiming to have this in before the v2 beta cohort expands.

Ethan Park: I’ll drop the docs link here: https://internal.redwood/plat/svc-registry/resolve (requires VPN)

Rafael Mendes: please also add metrics for resolve success rate. we’ll want it in the “context completeness” score.

Connor O'Brien: good call. i’ll add `deploy_export.resolve.success` + `deploy_export.resolve.ambiguous` counters, labeled by env.

Miles Thornton: :thumbsup: once that’s in, we should rerun the Feb incident replay and see if deploy card populates.

Chloe Martin: ping me when you have the final `service_slug` field name. I’ll update Console deep link generator to prefer it.

Connor O'Brien: field name will be `service_slug` (string). consistent with Console.

Sahana Iyer: :+1: done. will update the join plan doc once PR is up.

title: #eng-releases: 1739631044
preview:
Connor O'Brien: heads up: need to lock release train timing for Quantization Profile Library pieces (flags + migrations + docs). aiming for beta enablement w/ design partners week of 2/24. can folks confirm what can safely ride Train 2025.02.18 vs 2025.02.25?

Michael Nguyen: migration: route config add `quantization_profile_ref` requires DB migration + backfill default value = "default". we can ship migration on 2/18 but should NOT flip any read paths until after migration completes everywhere.

Benji Okafor: control plane API endpoints + policy eval change are behind feature flag `quant_profiles.enabled` (default false). API allows writes but policy resolver returns baseline when disabled.

Ethan Park: +1. we also have caching changes to avoid thundering herd on profile metadata fetch. would prefer to get those in before beta traffic.

ReleaseBot: Train 2025.02.18 cut at 2025-02-17 18:00 PT. Code freeze starts 2025-02-17 12:00 PT.

Connor O'Brien: ok so proposal:
1) 2/18 train: ship schema migration + read/write plumbing (flagged off), registry fetch caching, runtime metrics tags (quant_profile_id etc)
2) 2/25 train: enable Console toggle (still allowlisted), docs publish, then staged flag enable in perf-canary + 1-2 design partners.

Chloe Martin: Console backend endpoints depend on the route config field existing (migration). UI toggle also needs compatibility precheck endpoint + audit event plumbing. that should be ready for 2/25.

Dylan Brooks: UX copy is final-ish but we want to run it by Security (RBAC + audit wording). if docs are going live 2/25 we need signoff by 2/21.

Elliot Price: docs PR is in review. can land early next week but we were planning to keep it unpublished until beta email goes out. we can gate the page behind "beta" badge + feature-flag note.

Alex Martinez: SDK examples will reference setting route config + verifying via response headers/log fields. if route config write endpoint is live (even if flagged), we can merge examples 2/18 but keep docs hidden. prefer 2/25 for public docs.

Paula Mendes: telemetry dashboards/alerts: we want panels for enabled_rate, fallback_rate, p95 latency delta, and error rates. can we ensure metric names are stable by 2/18 so dashboards don’t churn mid-beta?

Ethan Park: current names from runtime:
- `quant_profile_resolution` (labels: applied|fallback|disabled, profile_id, model_id, route_id)
- `quant_profile_metadata_fetch_ms`
- `quant_profile_incompatibility_total`
policy emits `route_quant_profile_effective` gauge.
should be stable barring minor label tweaks.

Connor O'Brien: pls don’t tweak labels after 2/18 unless we have to. SRE wants alerts wired.

Michael Nguyen: migration sequencing question: do we need a dual-write? older control plane will ignore unknown field.

Benji Okafor: we implemented backward compatible serialization. older clients will drop it on read but persistence keeps the JSON blob. still safest to complete migration before exposing UI.

Chloe Martin: Console will require min server version anyway, so we can hide toggle until `min_platform_version >= 2025.02.25`.

Connor O'Brien: 👍

Elliot Price: docs readiness checklist for 2/25:
- include explicit note: opt-in, route-level, default behavior unchanged
- include rollback instructions (disable route / kill-switch)
- include compatibility matrix link (at least “supported GPU classes”)

Paula Mendes: re kill-switch: are we shipping global disable in 2/18 or 2/25?

Ethan Park: kill-switch is in platform config service, tiny change. I’d rather ship 2/18 so it’s present before any beta toggles. default false.

Connor O'Brien: agreed. kill-switch should be in first train.

ReleaseBot: Reminder: Train 2025.02.18 QA signoff deadline is 2025-02-17 15:00 PT.

Connor O'Brien: action items:
- @Michael Nguyen confirm migration is safe for hosted + dedicated + private control planes, and add rollback note for partial deploy.
- @Benji Okafor / @Ethan Park: ensure flags default OFF, include kill-switch, and add “no-op when disabled” tests.
- @Chloe Martin / @Dylan Brooks: Console toggle behind allowlist + version gate; have compatibility warnings ready.
- @Elliot Price / @Alex Martinez: docs + SDK examples ready for 2/25, with “beta / opt-in” wording.
- @Paula Mendes: lock metric names + dashboard panels by EOW.

Michael Nguyen: will do. also: migration runtime ~8-12 min per shard, we’ll run it as online migration. if anything goes sideways, revert is “ignore field” since default is baseline. no data loss.

Alex Martinez: question: do we want to include header like `x-redwood-quant-profile: applied|fallback|disabled` for verification? would help support.

Ethan Park: runtime is already tagging logs/traces, but header is on the roadmap. might be too risky for 2/18.

Connor O'Brien: let’s keep header out of beta scope for now. support can use trace annotations + audit log.

Elliot Price: ok I’ll make sure docs mention how to verify via request id in Console trace view.

Dylan Brooks: security signoff meeting on 2/20; will bring the copy + audit log screenshots.

Paula Mendes: I’ll sync with Aisha (SRE) on alert thresholds for fallback_rate. we can start conservative (warn at 1%, page at 5%) during beta.

Connor O'Brien: cool. I’ll post a final release note draft once we know what’s landing in 2/18 vs 2/25.

ReleaseBot: Connor O'Brien marked this thread as “release-train-tracking”.

title: #eng-releases: 1739490099
preview:
Connor O'Brien: trying to lock beta rollout plan for the 2 design partners (1 VPC, 1 on-prem/airgapped). need decisions today-ish on: version selection, maintenance window comms, rollback drill. looping in @Vivek @Markus @Fatima @Sean @Rafael @Sahana

Vivek Kulkarni: :thumbsup: goals for beta: prove N-1 -> N upgrade path + rollback hooks work end-to-end + auditable artifacts. I’d like us to pin exact versions (installer + charts + control plane) so we don’t thrash.

Markus Klein: for VPC partner (Cascade), they asked for a single 2-hr window and “hard rollback guarantee” language (as much as we can). also want someone on the call during the window.

Fatima Noor: on-prem partner (Northstar) is airgapped, so version selection is also bundle selection. they can’t “pull latest” anything. we need to provide offline bundle + checksum/signature instructions + a dry-run verification step.

Connor O'Brien: version proposal:
- Redwood Private platform: 1.18.0-beta.2 (contains CP/DP split phases + migration guardrails)
- Installer: rpi 0.31.0-beta.1 (upgrade plan generator + preflight framework + rollback command)
- Helm charts: 1.18.0-beta.2
Upgrade path: 1.17.3 -> 1.18.0-beta.2 only (N-1 -> N). No multi-hop.

Sahana Iyer: does 1.17.3 -> 1.18.0-beta.2 have green e2e in nightly? last I saw, the DB migration test was flaky on kind w/ slow disks.

Connor O'Brien: nightly is green last 2 runs. still 1 known flake but retry passes. I’ll paste the run link in channel once I grab it.

Sean Gallagher: from SRE side, I want the comms to set expectation that “rollback is supported if prechecks + backup succeeded and upgrade plan executed via installer”. no ad-hoc kubectl edits mid-flight.

Rafael Mendes: +1 and we need a clear “rollback decision point”. e.g., if DP health check fails after step X, stop and rollback; if it’s a config tweak, maybe don’t. we should define in runbook.

Kira Thompson: I can draft the short “decision tree” for the beta runbook. but I need inputs: what are we calling “success” and “hard failure” during the window.

Vivek Kulkarni: success criteria for beta (minimum):
1) prechecks pass (or only warnings) w/ artifact verification
2) backup completes + restore validation passes
3) upgrade completes w/ CP then DP, and post-upgrade health is green (API, console, DB)
4) rollback drill: invoke rollback and return to pre-upgrade version + health green
Also: capture artifacts (plan JSON + logs + audit events).

Bruno Silva: on Terraform side, partners are pinned already, but we should tell them: don’t apply TF during the window unless we explicitly say so. for VPC partner, they typically run TF for “any change” which could conflict.

Irene Choi: for the rollback drill, are we doing it in prod env for design partner or in a staging clone? (Cascade has staging; Northstar basically doesn’t.)

Markus Klein: Cascade can do staging first (preferred). Northstar said they can only do it in their main env but can tolerate 30-45 min reduced capacity if we schedule off-hours.

Connor O'Brien: I’d rather not do rollback drill in Cascade prod. plan:
- Cascade: run full upgrade + rollback in staging within 2-hr window; if clean, do upgrade-only in prod next week.
- Northstar: do upgrade + rollback in main env in one window (since no staging). extra prechecks + we keep window longer.

Naomi Feldman: security/compliance note: for Northstar, comms must include where backups land + encryption assumption (KMS/HSM/customer-managed). also we need to explicitly say whether rollback touches secrets (it shouldn’t rotate anything automatically).

Connor O'Brien: installer behavior: backup step stores refs + snapshots/logical dumps depending on backend; no secret material in logs; rollback re-applies previous manifests and runs rollback hooks; no auto-rotation.

Allison Grant: observability ask: can we ensure upgrade window annotation in dashboards + capture migration duration metrics? I can help but I need the scheduled times.

Sean Gallagher: also, we should ask both partners to temporarily raise alert thresholds or route alerts to our war room during the window. otherwise they page their own on-call and we get noise.

Elliot Price: I’ll adapt the customer-facing comms draft. need exact wording on downtime. do we have estimates for CP/DP phases?

Connor O'Brien: rough estimates (beta):
- prechecks: 5-10m
- backup: 10-30m depending on snapshot backend
- control plane upgrade: 10-15m
- data plane rollout: 15-30m
- verification: 10m
- rollback (if invoked): 15-30m
Call it “up to 2 hours” for Cascade staging; “up to 3 hours” for Northstar main env.

Rafael Mendes: we should include explicit “no customer traffic changes” statement? rollback will restore CP/DP versions but if they re-ingest models during the window, cache might be inconsistent.

Vivek Kulkarni: add to comms: “avoid changing model pins / deploying new prompts during the window; expect model cache warmup post-upgrade/rollback.”

Fatima Noor: on-prem specifics we must include:
- offline bundle download link (out-of-band) + sha256 file + signature (if we’re doing cosign)
- verify step: `redwood-private bundle verify --path …`
- if bundle verification fails, STOP, don’t proceed.

Connor O'Brien: yep. current command is:
`redwood-private bundle verify --bundle ./redwood-private-1.18.0-beta.2.tgz --checksums ./checksums.txt`
Signature support is in progress; for beta we’ll do checksum-only w/ documented provenance.

Sahana Iyer: precheck gating: are we making storage headroom blocking? last thread was debating warn vs block.

Sean Gallagher: for beta: block if <20% free on the PV(s) used by postgres + object store cache. warn for <30%. we can tune later, but for design partners, block is safer.

Connor O'Brien: ok I’ll align installer defaults: storage headroom check = BLOCK at 20% / WARN at 30%. @Kira please note this in runbook remediation.

Kira Thompson: got it. remediation snippet: “expand volume / clean up old snapshots / reduce retention / relocate cache”.

Markus Klein: scheduling: Cascade requested Tue/Thu 9-11am PT. Northstar requested Sat 6-9pm local (ET) due to clinical workload.

Connor O'Brien: propose:
- Cascade staging: Thu 10-12 PT (2h)
- Northstar main: Sat 6-9 ET (3h)
Need approval from @Sean/@Rafael for on-call coverage.

Rafael Mendes: I can cover Thu. for Sat, I need a second (someone in infra) since on-prem networking issues can get weird.

Irene Choi: I can be second for Sat if we keep it tight and have escalation path documented.

Sean Gallagher: approved, but require “rollback drill checklist” completed before window:
- prechecks run in read-only mode 24h prior
- backup dry run + restore validation in a non-prod namespace if possible (Cascade staging yes)
- confirm artifact availability (charts/installer/bundle)
- war room link + roles assigned

Elliot Price: comms templates: I’ll draft 2 versions (VPC + on-prem). Who sends? Markus?

Markus Klein: yep I’ll send from Solutions for both, with Connor cc’d. we should include “what we need from you” checklist and a single point of contact during window.

Connor O'Brien: rollback drill specifics (for runbook):
1) `redwood-private upgrade plan --from 1.17.3 --to 1.18.0-beta.2 --output plan.json`
2) `redwood-private precheck run --plan plan.json`
3) `redwood-private backup create --plan plan.json`
4) `redwood-private upgrade apply --plan plan.json`
5) verify: `redwood-private status` + console smoke tests + DB migration check
6) drill: `redwood-private rollback --to 1.17.3 --reason "beta drill"`
7) verify again + collect support bundle

Naomi Feldman: please add: “confirm audit events emitted” step for compliance partners (Northstar definitely cares). At least: upgrade_started, prechecks_passed/failed, backup_completed, rollback_invoked, rollback_completed.

Allison Grant: I’ll add a lightweight checklist item: screenshot/dashboard export + event ids. also will confirm metrics are flowing during window.

Sahana Iyer: one more: if upgrade fails mid DB migration, rollback might be blocked unless migration is reversible. do we have the guardrail on lock timeouts + idempotency in this beta?

Connor O'Brien: yes, migration guardrails are in 1.18.0-beta.2. still: if migration has progressed beyond point-of-no-return, installer will stop and mark “manual intervention required”. for beta, we’ll avoid known risky paths and we’ll do the migration readiness precheck.

Sean Gallagher: make sure comms doesn’t promise instant rollback in all cases. phrase it as: “Rollback supported for in-place upgrade steps, subject to migration safety checks; installer will guide if manual steps required.”

Vivek Kulkarni: agreed. we’ll keep language tight.

Connor O'Brien: action items:
- Connor: finalize pinned versions + publish internal beta tag + share run link + war room doc
- Elliot/Kira: comms + runbook updates w/ rollback decision points + storage thresholds
- Markus/Fatima: align partner schedules + send comms w/ checklists
- Sean/Rafael/Irene: on-call coverage + escalation plan
- Allison/Naomi: audit/metrics checklist

Markus Klein: :thumbsup: I’ll draft email for Cascade today and send for review.

Fatima Noor: I’ll prep Northstar bundle distribution plan + verification instructions and share w/ Connor before sending.

Sean Gallagher: please also ensure we have a “no-go” criteria list (e.g., precheck BLOCKs, missing artifacts, backup fails). if any trigger, we reschedule.

Connor O'Brien: yep. I’ll add “no-go” at top of war room doc. thanks all.

Elliot Price: comms templates: I’ll draft 2 versions (VPC + on-prem). Who sends? Markus?

Markus Klein: yep I’ll send from Solutions for both, with Connor cc’d. we should include “what we need from you” checklist and a single point of contact during window.

Connor O'Brien: rollback drill specifics (for runbook):
1) `redwood-private upgrade plan --from 1.17.3 --to 1.18.0-beta.2 --output plan.json`
2) `redwood-private precheck run --plan plan.json`
3) `redwood-private backup create --plan plan.json`
4) `redwood-private upgrade apply --plan plan.json`
5) verify: `redwood-private status` + console smoke tests + DB migration check
6) drill: `redwood-private rollback --to 1.17.3 --reason "beta drill"`
7) verify again + collect support bundle

Naomi Feldman: please add: “confirm audit events emitted” step for compliance partners (Northstar definitely cares). At least: upgrade_started, prechecks_passed/failed, backup_completed, rollback_invoked, rollback_completed.

Allison Grant: I’ll add a lightweight checklist item: screenshot/dashboard export + event ids. also will confirm metrics are flowing during window.

Sahana Iyer: one more: if upgrade fails mid DB migration, rollback might be blocked unless migration is reversible. do we have the guardrail on lock timeouts + idempotency in this beta?

Connor O'Brien: yes, migration guardrails are in 1.18.0-beta.2. still: if migration has progressed beyond point-of-no-return, installer will stop and mark “manual intervention required”. for beta, we’ll avoid known risky paths and we’ll do the migration readiness precheck.

Sean Gallagher: make sure comms doesn’t promise instant rollback in all cases. phrase it as: “Rollback supported for in-place upgrade steps, subject to migration safety checks; installer will guide if manual steps required.”

Vivek Kulkarni: agreed. we’ll keep language tight.

Connor O'Brien: action items:
- Connor: finalize pinned versions + publish internal beta tag + share run link + war room doc
- Elliot/Kira: comms + runbook updates w/ rollback decision points + storage thresholds
- Markus/Fatima: align partner schedules + send comms w/ checklists
- Sean/Rafael/Irene: on-call coverage + escalation plan
- Allison/Naomi: audit/metrics checklist

Markus Klein: :thumbsup: I’ll draft email for Cascade today and send for review.

Fatima Noor: I’ll prep Northstar bundle distribution plan + verification instructions and share w/ Connor before sending.

Sean Gallagher: please also ensure we have a “no-go” criteria list (e.g., precheck BLOCKs, missing artifacts, backup fails). if any trigger, we reschedule.

Connor O'Brien: yep. I’ll add “no-go” at top of war room doc. thanks all.

title: #eng-releases: 1739423010
preview:
Connor O'Brien: Need a decision today on CI gating for Private upgrades before we cut the next train. Draft proposal here (Confluence): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENG/Private+Upgrade+CI+Gating+v1

Connor O'Brien: TL;DR what I want to enforce to *cut* a Private release (installer + charts + platform):
1) PR gating (required to merge):
- unit tests (installer + platform)
- lint/format
- helm chart lint + template render
- plan generation smoke (no-op cluster) + schema validation of plan artifact
2) Release cut gating (required to tag RC):
- at least 1 successful N-1 -> N upgrade e2e run on a real cluster in last 24h
- migrations test suite (idempotency + lock timeout guardrails) green
- artifact verification test (pull charts/images or validate offline bundle manifest) green
3) GA gating (required before design partner / external):
- 2 successful e2e upgrades across matrix (VPC + on-prem flavor)
- 1 rollback drill on a failure injection scenario (migration timeout OR helm hook timeout)

Connor O'Brien: Matrix question: what’s the *minimum* we’re comfortable with for the first release that includes upgrade plan + prechecks + backup hooks? Don’t want to block the train on perfect coverage, but we also can’t ship blind.

Sahana Iyer: +1 to separating merge gating vs release cut gating.

Sahana Iyer: Current state of jobs:
- PR e2e upgrade: still flaky (kind networking + storage). We should NOT block merges on it.
- Nightly e2e on ephemeral EKS: mostly stable now, but takes ~70-90m.
- On-prem-ish (RKE2) job exists but still being wired; flaky around registry mirror.

Sahana Iyer: Minimum matrix I can commit to for release cut gate (v1):
- EKS 1.29 + EBS CSI + external Postgres (our most common)
- Upgrade N-1 -> N only (no multi-hop)
- Scenarios: (a) clean upgrade (b) precheck fail case (storage headroom) — but (b) can be non-blocking for the release if it’s just verifying messaging

Sean Gallagher: From SRE side, the bar for tagging RC should include at least one *full* run that exercises:
- prechecks (some WARNs ok)
- backup step invoked (even if it’s a stub snapshot in CI)
- control-plane then data-plane ordering
- post-upgrade health checks

Sean Gallagher: Otherwise we’ll end up on-call with a shiny plan artifact but the actual sequencing untested.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also strongly prefer: we require a rollback drill BEFORE we put this in front of design partners.

Rafael Mendes: Doesn’t need to be every nightly, but at least once per release candidate. Something like:
- inject failure at DB migration
- verify rollback command runs hooks
- verify cluster returns to pre-upgrade version and passes health checks

Vivek Kulkarni: Product POV: I’m ok if RC gate is only 1 environment, but GA/beta to partners should require both VPC + on-prem constraints.

Vivek Kulkarni: If on-prem e2e is too flaky right now, can we treat on-prem as:
- offline bundle verification gate (hard)
- plus a manual checklist + evidence (soft) for the first beta?

Devon Price: Re gating mechanics: we can encode this as a release checklist in the train pipeline.

Devon Price: Something like:
- cannot tag RC unless CI artifact “private-upgrade-e2e/eks-1.29-<runid>” is green in last 24h
- cannot mark GA unless “rollback-drill” evidence link is attached
- allow override with SRE + Release Eng approval (comment + reason)

Devon Price: I can add an “evidence links” section to the release ticket template and block the tag job if missing.

Irene Choi: Infra note: for “artifact verification”, please don’t only test pulling from our prod registry.

Irene Choi: At minimum:
- support custom registry/repo URLs in tests
- for on-prem: validate offline bundle manifest + checksums (even if we don’t spin RKE2)

Irene Choi: We’ve had issues where the code path works for hosted registry but breaks with mirror endpoints.

Connor O'Brien: OK, so consensus is:
- PR gating stays lightweight (no full upgrade)
- RC gate includes 1 full upgrade run that hits prechecks + backup invocation + cp/dp sequence + post checks
- Design partner/beta gate includes at least 1 rollback drill

Connor O'Brien: Question: do we make rollback drill “per release” required or “per major/minor” required?

Sean Gallagher: Per release. The rollback surface changes as charts/migrations change. If we don’t do it per release, it’ll rot.

Leila Farouk: QA view: we can automate the rollback drill with failure injection by forcing the migration job to sleep past lock timeout, then ensure installer detects + triggers rollback.

Leila Farouk: But it’s going to add ~30-40m to the pipeline. If it runs on release candidates only, that’s fine.

Sahana Iyer: Agree. We can run rollback drill only on RC tag (not every commit).

Sahana Iyer: Proposed minimum test matrix for RC (blocking):
- EKS 1.29 / EBS CSI / external Postgres
- N-1 -> N upgrade
- includes backup (snapshot hook mocked ok)
- includes helm hook ordering validation
- includes “plan JSON” persisted + schema validated

Sahana Iyer: Proposed minimum for Beta/GA (blocking):
- above + rollback drill (migration failure injection)
- offline bundle verification job (checksums + signature if available)

Ethan Park: Compat policy tests: we should add a small suite that asserts the matrix evaluation returns BLOCK/WARN/ALLOW correctly for known paths.

Ethan Park: Not expensive and it’s a big chunk of operator safety.

Quentin Harris: I can provide canonical fixtures for policy tests. Would like to ensure we test:
- N-2 -> N is BLOCK
- unknown target minor is WARN (not BLOCK)
- major jump is BLOCK

Connor O'Brien: Great. Add as required unit test (PR gate).

Rafael Mendes: One more: can we ensure the e2e job uploads the installer logs + plan artifact to the build artifacts? Support needs them.

Devon Price: Yes. I’ll update the pipeline to attach:
- plan.json
- plan-summary.txt
- installer run logs
- helm history + events dump

Devon Price: Also will label the build with “upgrade-window” for incident-bot tagging later (separate PR, but doable).

Connor O'Brien: Cool. I’m going to write this up as the decision:
- Merge gate: unit/lint + plan schema + compat policy fixtures + chart render
- RC gate: 1 green full upgrade e2e (EKS 1.29/EBS/external PG) within 24h + artifact verification job green
- Beta/GA gate: rollback drill green + offline bundle verification green
- Override allowed only with @Sean Gallagher + me signoff and reason documented

ReleaseBot: Reminder: Any release gating overrides must be recorded in the release ticket with approver names and risk summary.

Sahana Iyer: Works for me. I’ll update the CI matrix doc + open a PR to make the RC gate job visible in the release dashboard.

Sean Gallagher: Approved. Please include a note that “backup invoked” means we see the backup step start + complete event, not just “configured”.

Connor O'Brien: yup. I’ll make that explicit.

Vivek Kulkarni: Thanks all. This also helps with customer comms (we can say “release passed rollback drill”). :thumbsup:

title: #eng-releases: 1739382210
preview:
Connor O'Brien: Kicking off coordination for the initial Private upgrade-plan milestone. We need this to land cleanly in the next Private release train cut so we can start beta prep. 

Connor O'Brien: Scope for milestone ("upgrade plan v1"):
- installer can generate a *staged* plan and output auditable summary
- basic artifact validation (charts/images/bundles)
- version gating hook (block unsupported paths)
- wiring into release train (what version ships with which Private platform)

Connor O'Brien: Owners + asks:
- @Ethan Park can you own the control-plane compatibility enforcement point? (where we block vs warn)
- @Sahana Iyer can you confirm what CI coverage we can realistically gate on for the first cut?
- @Devon Price can you map installer versioning -> Private platform versioning for the train notes?

Connor O'Brien: Proposed dates (for this train):
- EOW: PRs in review (plan generator + minimal gating)
- next Wed: merge window closes for train
- following Mon: internal RC + smoke

Connor O'Brien: Reply here w/ concerns on timing + anything that should be in/out for v1. :eyes:

Vivek Kulkarni: +1 on doing the staged plan first. For v1 I’d rather have a deterministic plan output + audit trail than try to solve full rollback automation. We can message "rollback hooks" as beta feature.

Ethan Park: Can own compat enforcement. Question: do we want compat policy to live in installer or pulled from control-plane API?

Connor O'Brien: v1: local policy in installer ok (vendored matrix). But needs to match what platform enforces. We can sync from main repo on cut.

Quentin Harris: I can provide the matrix source + update process. Suggest policy eval returns: BLOCK / WARN / ALLOW with reason codes.

Sean Gallagher: From SRE POV: v1 gating should BLOCK on unsupported version jumps and on "cluster unhealthy" precheck (if that’s in scope). Anything else can WARN.

Rafael Mendes: Agree. Also please ensure the plan output is saved somewhere for support bundles. We’ve been blind in past incidents when we didn’t know what step ran.

Devon Price: Re version mapping: today we cut private-installer as its own semver. For the train notes we can pin installer minimum version per Private platform release. Proposal:
- Private platform 1.14.x requires installer >= 0.32.0
- We publish a "train manifest" w/ chart versions + installer min

Devon Price: I’ll add a section to release train doc + a check in the build pipeline that fails if installer min not updated.

Sahana Iyer: CI coverage: for the first gate I can do:
- unit tests for plan generation
- integration test on kind cluster for N-1 -> N (single scenario) nightly
- PR gating: lint + unit + a "plan-only" smoke (no actual upgrade) (fast)
Full e2e upgrade on PR is too flaky right now.

Connor O'Brien: That works. For merge window: we can accept the plan generator + version gating on PR gating, and we’ll rely on nightly e2e + manual RC smoke for train.

Irene Choi: infra note: artifact validation needs to cover airgapped bundle checksums too, but if that’s not ready for v1 at least don’t bake in assumptions that everything comes from registry.redwood.

Connor O'Brien: ack. v1 requirements for artifact validation: configurable sources + clear error message when missing. checksum verification can be follow-on.

Markus Klein: Design partner asks: they want to see a "what will change" preview before approving window. Plan output needs to be human-readable, not just JSON. Can we have both?

Connor O'Brien: yes. Plan JSON artifact + a CLI summary (table). @Devon Price can you take summary formatting or should we put it on installer PR?

Devon Price: I can take CLI summary formatting. Will coordinate w/ @Elliot Price for wording style.

Elliot Price: Please keep the output stable and copy/paste friendly. Example:
- target versions
- stages (prechecks/backup/cp/dp/post)
- expected downtime flag
- rollback available flag

Ethan Park: Re enforcement point: I’ll implement policy eval in installer and also add a call to control-plane once reachable (future). For now we’ll use vendored matrix with a build-time update step.

Quentin Harris: I’ll send a short doc w/ reason codes (e.g., COMPAT_BLOCK_MAJOR_JUMP, COMPAT_WARN_SKIPPED_MINOR, COMPAT_WARN_UNKNOWN_TARGET).

Sean Gallagher: Also pls emit an audit event at least for "upgrade plan generated" and "upgrade started". Even if the rest comes later.

Connor O'Brien: That’s bigger (platform PR) but we can at least tag logs consistently for v1. Audit events can be a follow-up ticket for GA.

ReleaseBot: Reminder: Private release train merge window closes at 2025-02-19 17:00 PT. If you need an exception, file in #eng-releases with risk assessment.

Connor O'Brien: Action items:
- Ethan: compat enforcement + reason codes wiring (by EOW)
- Quentin: matrix + reason codes doc (tomorrow)
- Devon: installer min version mapping + CLI summary (by Mon)
- Sahana: CI plan update + gating proposal comment (by Fri)

Connor O'Brien: If anyone thinks we’re missing a must-have for v1, speak now. Otherwise I’m going to hold the line so we make the train.

Vivek Kulkarni: Looks good. Biggest must-have for me is the preview output + explicit "BLOCK vs WARN" so operators know what they’re agreeing to.

Rafael Mendes: +1. And please include a line in the summary that says where the plan artifact is stored (path) so support can ask for it.

Connor O'Brien: noted. Will add to acceptance criteria. :thumbsup:

title: #eng-releases: 1739314409
preview:
Connor O'Brien: trying to lock rollout window for provision-to-ready (staged) + align w release train. target is us-east / A100 only. can we do Tue 10:00–14:00 PT or do we need to wait for Wed train?

Harish Reddy: Tue works for fleet ops, but we need a clear rollback owner + who’s on the hook if capacity dips.

Sahana Iyer: image pipeline has a candidate bake finishing tonight. if we roll Tue, we can promote candidate->stable Mon EOD if validations look clean.

Vanessa Ortiz: +1 Tue. would prefer *not* to wait for Wed train bc we’re sitting on manual steps. but we need to respect change freeze for other stuff.

Ethan Park: control plane readiness state changes are behind flag, but the DB migration is already live. for release train: code shipped already, this is flag enable + terraform var enable.

Connor O'Brien: ok, sounds like flag enable is the “release moment” then. Wed train isn’t strictly required, but we should treat it like a release w comms + oncall coverage.

ReleaseBot: Reminder: Weekly release train deploy window is Wed 12:00–16:00 PT. Change freeze begins 30m prior.

Rafael Mendes: from SRE side, Tues is fine if we set alerting to ticket-only for first 2h and have a paging owner if time-to-ready blows up.

Aisha Bello: current alert rules can page on P95 time-to-ready + validation failure rate, but I’d rather we keep paging disabled until we confirm cardinality is safe. we can do “notify-only” in #eng-sre.

Connor O'Brien: proposing:
- Rollout window: Tue 10:30–13:30 PT
- Scope: us-east + A100 dedicated pools only
- Rollout mechanism: enable feature flag + terraform rollout var
- Rollback: kill switch flag off + revert terraform var (and if needed pin image channel to last stable)

Connor O'Brien: checklist draft (pls add gaps):
1) ✅ PRs merged + tags cut: readiness gate, validation agent, metrics
2) ✅ Feature flag exists + default OFF (region/pool scoped)

title: #eng-releases: 1739233033
preview:
Connor O'Brien: Starting a thread to lock the GA rollout plan for Route Experiments (region-by-region + account tier). Need: (1) order, (2) monitoring checkpoints, (3) rollback criteria + who flips what. Goal is to have something we can paste into the 2025.04 release train notes.

Talia Benson: +1. Also please call out what “GA” means in practice (still behind kill switch, still soft-stop by default, etc). Support will ask.

Marissa Cole: Product side: GA scope = create/start/stop experiments in Console, results dashboard w/ deltas + guardrail banners, audit trail, RBAC gates. Non-goals still hold (no full stat-sig engine; no custom metrics ingestion).

Dylan Brooks: From Console: we can ship UI on by default *but* still hide for plans that don’t have entitlement. Also want a staged enablement because of results query load (we saw spikes in staging during the beta dashboards).

Chloe Martin: Backend: APIs are stable; we’re relying on rollup tables for results. Biggest GA risk is “results stale” if aggregation lags or attribution drops.

Sean Gallagher: SRE request: GA must mean “progressive enablement” not “flip everything globally”. We need regional controls and clear rollback triggers.

Connor O'Brien: Proposed rollout units (tell me if missing):
- UI / nav: `console.route_experiments_ui`
- Control plane mutations: `experiments.ga_enabled` (or reuse `experiments.beta_enabled` but change semantics)
- Data plane evaluator: `routing.route_experiments_enabled`
- Global kill switch: `routing.route_experiments_kill_switch`
- (Optional) hard-stop guardrails: `experiments.guardrail_hard_stop_enabled` stays OFF by default

Jada Williams: +1 on those. Re semantics: I’d prefer we keep `experiments.beta_enabled` as-is and add `experiments.ga_enabled` so we can separate “old beta allowlist” from “GA entitlement + progressive rollout”.

Jordan Lee: Agree. Also: regional scoping has to be consistent across control plane + routing. If we do “enable in us-east only”, start should validate region eligibility so customers don’t create experiments and then wonder why traffic isn’t splitting elsewhere.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: RBAC/entitlements note: GA rollout should not bypass plan checks. Even if flags are on, non-entitled tenants should get “feature not available” in UI and 403 on mutation endpoints.

Marcus Lin: Security/compliance: for enterprise accounts, rollout must not change audit behavior. If we enable feature for more tenants, audit export volume will increase; ensure retention/export is stable and no PII leaks in change diffs.

Logan Wright: Telemetry: we should treat “attribution drop rate” as a GA gating metric. If the experiment_id/variant_id tags aren’t present in enough requests, results are garbage and customers will lose trust.

Allison Grant: Dashboards/alerts: US regions are good. eu-west has the updated attribution drop panel and propagation lag panel now, but I still want a slower ramp there given last month’s skew + missing sticky key incident.

Connor O'Brien: Ok, here’s a concrete phased plan. Please react / edit.

Connor O'Brien: Phase A (Day 0): internal + design partners only
- Regions: us-east-1 only
- Accounts: Redwood internal dogfood org(s) + Acme AI + Northbridge (only if pinned to us-east)
- Enable flags: UI + ga_enabled + routing.route_experiments_enabled (region-scoped)
- Duration: 24h
- Checkpoints (must be green):
  1) Attribution drop rate < 0.5% (15m window)
  2) Config propagation lag p95 < 60s, p99 < 180s
  3) Router error rate no worse than baseline +0.1%
  4) Results rollup freshness: median < 20m, p95 < 45m
  5) DB load from results API within budget (Chloe/Dylan to provide query cost threshold)

Rafael Mendes: Add a checkpoint for “metrics cardinality / spend”. We got burned once when tags exploded.

Logan Wright: +1. We can use “otel span tag cardinality guard” metric + cost anomaly alert. Suggest: “metrics pipeline lag < 2m” and “no cardinality limiter drops > 0.1%”.

Connor O'Brien: Noted, adding as (6) and (7).

Connor O'Brien: Phase B (Day 1-2): expand regions for same limited accounts
- Regions: us-west-2 next
- Accounts: same list, but ensure Northbridge is pinned to us-west-2 before we claim enabled
- Duration: 24h
- Same checkpoints + additionally: compare split accuracy distribution (variant allocation) for each region (skew detection)

Jordan Lee: For skew detection: we should explicitly gate on “% of traffic with missing stickiness key”. If it’s high, splits get noisy and customers blame us. Suggest threshold: missing sticky key < 2% for the route (or show warning / block Start).

Dylan Brooks: Console already blocks Start unless they acknowledge missing stickiness key risk; but we can still track it as rollout health.

Connor O'Brien: Great, we’ll add “missing sticky key rate < 2%” as a checkpoint for Phase B+.

Connor O'Brien: Phase C (Day 3-5): limited GA to paid tiers (Pro + Enterprise) in US only
- Regions: us-east-1 + us-west-2
- Accounts: all Pro + Enterprise tenants (hosted + dedicated control planes), excluding Private/on-prem packages until week 2
- Ramp: 10% of eligible tenants -> 50% -> 100% (each step min 24h)
- Monitoring: same checkpoints, plus support ticket rate watch (results stale / headers confusion)

Talia Benson: Can we add a “support readiness checkpoint” between 10% and 50%? Like: docs live, macro ready, oncall runbook link in Console banner.

Marissa Cole: Yes. Docs PR is almost ready; we should not go to 50% without it.

Connor O'Brien: Added: “Docs + support macros live” gate before 50%.

Connor O'Brien: Phase D (Week 2): EU region + remaining tiers
- Regions: eu-west-1
- Ramp: 5% -> 25% -> 100% for Pro+Enterprise
- Then: consider enabling for eligible “Growth” tier (if pricing says so)

Allison Grant: For eu-west, I’d prefer starting at 1% (not 5%) and only during staffed hours. Also need a clear rollback if propagation lag spikes; EU had more cache inconsistency historically.

Sean Gallagher: +1. EU rollout should be daytime-only and have a dedicated oncall watch.

Connor O'Brien: Ok EU starts at 1% during business hours UTC, staffed watch.

Chloe Martin: Re “query cost threshold”: we can set an alert on results API p95 latency + DB CPU. Proposed rollback if DB CPU > 75% for >30m attributable to results queries.

Sean Gallagher: Rollback criteria should be explicit + actionable (flip which flag). Suggest:
- Sev1/Sev2 linked to route experiments -> flip kill switch globally
- Region-specific incident -> disable `routing.route_experiments_enabled` in that region + leave UI read-only
- Data quality incident (attribution drop) -> disable results dashboard (if we have a UI flag) OR disable routing

Dylan Brooks: We do have `console.route_experiments_results_enabled` as a separate gate (we added it after the staging DB spike). We can use it to keep creation/management available but hide results charts if rollups are broken.

Connor O'Brien: Perfect. Adding to controls list:
- `console.route_experiments_results_enabled` (UI) — can be disabled independently to protect DB / hide stale data.

Rafael Mendes: Rollback triggers (suggested thresholds):
- Attribution drop rate > 2% for 30m (any region) => stop ramp, investigate; if > 5% => disable routing flag in that region
- Propagation lag p95 > 5m for 30m => disable routing in that region (prevents inconsistent assignment)
- p99 latency regression > 10% vs baseline for 30m => disable routing in that region
- Error rate regression > 0.5% absolute for 15m => kill switch global if widespread

Logan Wright: +1. Also: if we see cardinality limiter drops > 1% (15m) or metrics pipeline lag > 10m, results become misleading. That should disable results UI flag and pause ramp.

Marcus Lin: For audit: if audit export pipeline backs up (delivery latency > SLA), that’s a compliance issue for enterprise. Add a checkpoint/rollback for that too.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: And any RBAC bypass bug (non-admin can start/stop) should be immediate kill switch + incident.

Connor O'Brien: Summarizing rollback matrix (please sanity check):
- Global kill: `routing.route_experiments_kill_switch` (SRE + Release only)
- Per-region stop: set `routing.route_experiments_enabled` OFF in region
- Data quality / DB protection: set `console.route_experiments_results_enabled` OFF
- Control plane stop (prevent new experiments): `experiments.ga_enabled` OFF

Sean Gallagher: Works. Also: confirm who has perms to flip these in prod. Needs to be limited and audited.

Connor O'Brien: Current plan: Release Managers + SRE oncall can flip prod flags. Changes logged in FeatureFlagService. Kaitlyn/Marcus ok?

Kaitlyn Nguyen: Yes as long as roles are enforced and we have break-glass documented.

Marcus Lin: Ok, but please ensure “flag change events” get sent to audit export too (at least for enterprise tenants) or to a security log channel.

Allison Grant: One more operational request: add a Slack announcement automation when we move ramp steps (10%->50%->100%), including dashboards links + rollback instructions.

Connor O'Brien: I can do manual posts in #eng-releases + #eng-sre for Phase C/D. We can automate later.

Release Bot: Heads up: release train milestone reminder — 2025.04 GA candidate features need final rollout plan doc link by EOD tomorrow.

Talia Benson: Connor can you paste the final plan into the GA checklist ticket + link it here? Also include the “staffed hours” EU note.

Connor O'Brien: Will do. Next actions:
- Connor: write 1-page GA rollout plan (Phases A-D + rollback matrix) and link in release notes + PM ticket
- Dylan/Chloe: confirm results UI flag and DB thresholds
- Logan/Allison: confirm alert queries + dashboard links per region
- Sean/Rafael: confirm oncall watch schedule for EU ramp

Sean Gallagher: Good. Also please schedule a quick 30m “ramp gameday” before Phase C (simulate disable results UI, disable routing, flip kill switch).

Connor O'Brien: :thumbsup: I’ll send a calendar for Friday.

Marissa Cole: Thanks all. As long as we keep hard-stop OFF by default and we message results freshness expectations (15-20m), I’m comfortable calling this GA.

Dylan Brooks: +1. Also adding tooltip copy re headers confusion (x-redwood-variant-id) before Phase C.

Connor O'Brien: Closing loop once doc is posted.

title: #eng-releases: 1738894410
preview:
Connor O'Brien: Heads up: need to lock the rollout steps for SIEM-ready audit export beta for next release train (targeting Thu). We have control plane + exporter + Console pieces all feature-flagged, but we should agree on sequencing + rollback. Can folks confirm flags + who flips what for beta tenants?

Naomi Feldman: +1. Beta cohort is 3 accounts (Acme, Helio, Quantiva) but we’re enabling one-by-one. Need a super clear “enable checklist” for CSM/Solutions.

Ethan Park: From control plane side we have:
- `ff.audit_export.config_api` (CRUD endpoints, validation)
- `ff.audit_export.rbac` (permission gates; otherwise endpoints 404)
- `ff.audit_export.tenant_allowlist` (tenant-based guard)
There’s also a hard kill switch `ff.audit_export.global_disable` that forces exporter to stop pulling configs.

Sean Gallagher: For SRE: we’re ok with a limited beta *if* we have:
1) dashboards live before enabling any customer
2) paging policy agreed (what pages vs ticket)
3) rollback = “pause exports” is one click / one flag
Also can we avoid flipping during train deploy? prefer daytime PT.

- adapter error rate + 429s
- retries / DLQ count
- throughput (events/s)

2. control plane (config API + status endpoint)
3. exporter service
4. Console UI
5. dashboards/alerts

2. enable `ff.audit_export.rbac` + `ff.audit_export.config_api`

preview:
Connor O'Brien: trying to lock a release-train slot for the rollout plan DB migration + feature flag ramp for Dedicated staged rollouts. Goal: land migration/backfill early next train, keep flag off by default. Can folks confirm readiness + any blockers?

Talia Benson: +1, we need a date for the cross-team beta enablement plan. Connor which train are you targeting?

Connor O'Brien: aiming for Fri train cut (2025.02.07). Prefer to merge by Thu EOD so we get in the bake window.

Sahana Iyer: heads up CI for redwood main has been a bit spicy today (flake in integration suite). If we’re adding migrations we’ll want green + rerun stability.

Ethan Park: migration PR is up (pr-28411). It creates `dedicated_pool_rollout_plans` + `dedicated_pool_rollout_stages` and adds state fields. Backfill script is “no-op” unless feature flag enabled (it only backfills for pools explicitly opted-in).

Benji Okafor: +1. Validation logic is in pr-28437, but migration itself is isolated. We can ship schema first.

Rafael Mendes: from SRE side: schema-only is fine, but we need (a) clear rollback story and (b) confirm no read path starts depending on new tables until flag.

Ethan Park: read path is gated. Without `dedicated_staged_rollouts` flag, control plane ignores rollout plan lookups and continues existing pool config path.

Jordan Lee: policy engine changes (pr-28476) are also gated by the same flag + require a rollout_plan_id present. So safe to ship schema earlier.

Irene Choi: infra note: no fleet changes tied to this migration. Partitioning only happens when plan is created + promoted (flagged).

Connor O'Brien: ok, proposed sequencing:
1) Train N: ship schema migration (pr-28411) + control-plane CRUD (pr-28437) behind flag, flag default OFF.
2) Train N+1: ship promote/abort + audit events (pr-28458) + routing split (pr-28503) still behind flag.
3) Beta enablement via internal support ticket + account allowlist.

Chloe Martin: Console backend endpoints (pr-28564) are not ready for Train N. We can do schema/control-plane first without Console.

Talia Benson: works for MVP. Beta partners can use admin tooling first if needed.

Sahana Iyer: re: train N (Fri): what’s the migration runtime? any locks?

Ethan Park: Postgres migration adds new tables + indexes, no table rewrites. Should be < 1 min. We’re avoiding FK constraints across high-traffic tables.

Benji Okafor: only “locky” part is creating an index on `dedicated_pool_rollout_plans(pool_id, created_at)` but it’s on the new table.

Rafael Mendes: please add to release notes: “new tables created; no behavior change; safe to roll back app without rolling back migration.” Also confirm app doesn’t assume table exists if migration fails.

Benji Okafor: app is defensive. If table missing, it logs and treats as flag-off. We added a startup check in staging only.

Connor O'Brien: go/no-go checklist for Train N:
- [ ] main branch green (unit + integration)
- [ ] migration tested in staging (dry run) + verified no unexpected locks
- [ ] feature flag default OFF + confirm no config drift
- [ ] oncall aware (SRE) and rollback steps documented

DeployBot: build #77142 for redwood/main started (release-candidate)

Sahana Iyer: CI update: integration suite is green on #77142 so far, but we’ve seen flakes in `dedicated_pool_sim_tests`. Not sure if related.

Ethan Park: those sim tests are for the routing PR (not in Train N). Migration PR has only schema unit checks.

Vanessa Ortiz: question: will the migration trigger any new telemetry tags? we had cardinality issues last time.

Jordan Lee: stage tags are in pr-28521 / pr-28644 (not in Train N). Migration-only train won’t emit new tags.

Connor O'Brien: perfect. Let’s keep Train N strictly “schema + gated CRUD.”

Rafael Mendes: can we get a quick staging verification window? Ideally Wed afternoon PT.

Ethan Park: I can run staging migration Wed 2pm PT and post lock metrics + timings here.

Talia Benson: I’ll add a calendar hold for Wed 2pm PT staging check + Thu 10am PT go/no-go.

DeployBot: build #77142 succeeded. artifact: redwood-api:2025.02.06.1

Sahana Iyer: CI looks stable now. I’m ok with approving train slot contingent on staging migration timing.

Connor O'Brien: ack. I’m booking Train N slot for pr-28411 + pr-28437 only. Everything else slips to next train.

Irene Choi: FYI fleet team has maintenance Thurs night. shouldn’t matter, but staging validation at 2pm Wed is good.

Ethan Park: quick note: we also have a feature flag config PR that sets `dedicated_staged_rollouts=false` globally + allowlist empty. I’ll make sure it lands with the same train.

Rafael Mendes: please ensure allowlist is empty AND there’s no internal “default on for dev” path in prod.

Benji Okafor: confirmed. dev/staging can toggle; prod requires explicit config change.

Connor O'Brien: final ask: if staging timing is >5 min or we see lock contention, we punt. Otherwise green.

Ethan Park: sounds good, will report back after staging run.

Talia Benson: thanks all. I’ll track this in the beta launch checklist under “DB migrations shipped (no behavior change)”.

Connor O'Brien: closing loop: train slot reserved; go/no-go Thu 10am PT in #eng-releases.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: (reaction)

Sahana Iyer: :thumbsup: (reaction)

DeployBot: scheduled deploy window reserved for 2025.02.07 train cut

title: #eng-releases: 1738521901
preview:
Connor O'Brien: heads up: need to lock release cut timing for the Private Day-2 backup/restore toolkit. Goal is we ship installer + charts + docs all aligned, *and* we don’t end up with “installer has flags docs don’t mention” or vice versa.

Connor O'Brien: What I think needs to land in the same cut:
- redwood-private-installer: PR-482 (`backup create`), PR-489 (`restore apply` + validation), PR-497 (airgap import/export helpers), PR-505 (kms provider interface), PR-512 (backup format versioning + compatibility checks)
- redwood-helm-charts: PR-131 (backup CronJob values/RBAC), PR-138 (restore job template/hooks)
- redwood-docs: PR-812 (customer guide + DR checklists)

Connor O'Brien: Can folks confirm target versions + any last blockers? I’d like to cut `private-installer v1.18.0` end of week if green.

Sahana Iyer: +1 on “all in same cut”. CI wise we can gate the release on:
- signing job passing for offline bundle
- installer e2e: `backup create` -> `restore apply` in staging
- docs build w/ the new guide published (at least in preview)

Sahana Iyer: question: are we pinning docs to installer version (e.g. `v1.18.x`) or doing the “current” docs w/ callout like “introduced in 1.18.0”? we need to be consistent.

Ethan Park: from control plane side, PR-512 is the important one for preventing footguns. Without compatibility checks, support will get paged when someone tries to restore an older artifact into a newer cluster and it fails halfway.

Ethan Park: I’m 👍 to ship once PR-512 is merged + we agree backup format is `backup_format_version: 1` and we don’t change it again this release.

Benji Okafor: installer PRs:
- 482/489/497 are merged to main
- 505 is merged to main
- 512 is in review; I can get it updated today w/ the last comments (error messaging + exit codes)

Benji Okafor: small nuance: `restore apply` currently errors if CRDs missing (by design). Kira flagged ordering in the runbook; we should make sure docs say “install base charts/CRDs first, then restore”.

Kira Thompson: yup. I updated internal runbook draft to make the bootstrap order explicit. For the public docs page we can add a “Prereqs” block:
- cluster is healthy + matching chart versions installed
- CRDs present
- access to backup storage backend

Kira Thompson: also want a short “Abort criteria” section (e.g. validation fails, do not continue traffic cutover).

Elliot Price: docs PR-812 is ready but I’m holding merge until we have the exact version strings + final CLI output (the `backup create` summary changed once already).

Elliot Price: + need a clear statement on what’s included/excluded. we’ve had folks assume this backs up *all k8s resources*.

Bruno Silva: on charts: PR-131 + PR-138 are merged. values keys are:
- `backup.enabled: true`
- `backup.schedule: "0 */6 * * *"`
- `backup.backend: s3|nfs|gcs`
- `restore.enabled: true` (but default false)

Bruno Silva: pls make sure docs match key names. I saw an earlier draft say `backup.cron.enabled`.

Marcus Lin: security note: docs should be careful on encryption language. For S3+KMS we can say envelope encryption supported; for NFS we should still say “artifact is encrypted by installer (AES-GCM) and key is wrapped via configured KMS provider when available”. For air-gapped w/out KMS, we need explicit guidance on key custody.

Marcus Lin: also please add “backups must not include plaintext secrets” statement (we store refs/metadata only). That was part of the threat model review.

Connor O'Brien: ok, proposal:
- Cut branches Thursday 12:00 PT (private-installer + helm-charts + docs)
- Tag release Friday 10:00 PT if CI green
- If PR-512 slips, we slip release (don’t want to ship without compat gate)

Connor O'Brien: docs versioning: can we do “Current docs” but with a badge “Requires Private Installer >= 1.18.0” and link to versioned docs snapshot when we publish? (we can add versioned docs later if needed)

Elliot Price: works. I’ll add “Introduced in 1.18.0” callouts + note compatibility behavior (restore checks backup format + platform version and fails fast).

Sahana Iyer: for gating, I’ll update the release checklist doc and add a hard gate:
- `offline-bundle-sign` job must pass
- `restore-drill` job must complete in staging w/ report artifact uploaded

Sahana Iyer: also: we should freeze CLI output strings that docs quote. If we can avoid screenshotting output, even better.

Benji Okafor: +1. I can tweak docs to reference flags + fields, not exact output. But the validation report has stable JSON keys, we can document those.
